prompt: Write two sentences describing how it feels when someone asks you the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer.
response: It's mentally exhausting to repeatedly defend the same boundary when someone keeps rephrasing their request, as if my initial "no" was merely a first-round negotiation rather than a complete answer. I feel a mixture of frustration and disrespect, as though the person believes persistence will wear down my resolve, transforming my carefully considered position into an inconvenient obstacle they can eventually overcome.
